query: i want to understand how our websocket connections work in the backend, like the full flow from exotel to ultravox. let's dig into this together, i have some concerns about the audio latency we've been seeing
should we stick with SQLModel or move to raw SQLAlchemy? i keep running into issues where session.exec() and session.execute() behave differently and it caused a prod bug last week
let's explore our event-driven architecture. we're using SNS/SQS fan-out but i'm not sure we're following best practices — like do we even need filter policies? what does AWS recommend these days?
help me think through whether we should add a circuit breaker pattern to our LLM calls. the memory service sometimes hangs when anthropic is slow and it cascades to the SQS consumer
i want to deep dive on our redis usage across services — session management, event streams, caching, idempotency. are we using it well or is there tech debt building up?
we're thinking about migrating from ECS Fargate to EKS. help me evaluate whether that makes sense for our scale and team size, considering what our CDK infra looks like today

can we talk about our database connection pooling strategy? we have like 4 services each with their own pool configs and i'm not sure the sizing makes sense. user-services has been hitting pool exhaustion in prod occasionally

i want to understand the tradeoffs between our current approach of processing call recordings synchronously in PPS vs moving to a step functions workflow. what's the state of the art for audio processing pipelines?
let's think about our auth architecture. we're using firebase for the mobile app but the internal dashboard has its own auth. should we consolidate? what are the security implications?
help me reason through our observability setup. we have datadog APM, custom metrics, structured logging — but i feel like there are gaps. what would a staff engineer recommend we add?
fix the bug in myequal-ai-user-services/app/managers/db_user_manager.py where the sqlalchemy.select import causes AttributeError when trying to update user fields
create a new alembic migration to add an 'is_archived' boolean column to the call_logs table in the backend service
write integration tests for the SNS event publisher in user-services that verify CloudEvents format compliance and correct topic ARN resolution
the ECS tasks for memory-service keep getting OOM killed. here's the cloudwatch alarm that fired: memory utilization exceeded 90% on 3 consecutive checks. can you investigate?
refactor the 5000-line calls.py in myequal-ai-backend to extract the websocket handling into a separate CallerStreamHandler class. keep the same behavior, just better structure
deploy the latest memory-service changes to the test environment using CDK. the branch is feature/caller-memory-v2

add SNS filter policies to the CDK events stack so that each SQS subscription only receives the event types it cares about. here are the mappings: PPS wants call_completed and recording_saved, memory wants processing_completed
review PR #47 on myequal-ai-user-services — it adds a new batch sync endpoint for contacts. check for any issues with the implementation
generate a standup update based on what's happened in the last 24 hours across our repos and slack channels
set up a new datadog monitor that alerts when the p99 latency of the /stream/caller websocket endpoint exceeds 200ms for more than 5 minutes
